frame_analyses.description: Suture already threaded on needle driver. Right hand (dominant) grasping needle driver with needle visible. Left hand holding opposite driver. Needles appear ready for suture placement through drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle positioned for immediate entry. Drivers are in bilateral symmetric position.

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver advancing needle into entry point of slit on Penrose drain. Needle angle appears oblique to drain surface.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle entry appears to be at angle rather than perpendicular. Entry point location relative to marked line is difficult to assess from this view.

frame_analyses.description: Needle passing through drain slit. Both needle drivers visible, maintaining tension on the suture. Penetration appears complete.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good instrument coordination. Suture threading through drain appears smooth.

frame_analyses.description: Needle exiting second mark on drain. Suture now spanning both entry and exit points. Left hand positioning needle driver for exit.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Exit point alignment with marked target cannot be clearly confirmed from angle.

frame_analyses.description: Needle fully extracted. Suture now threaded through drain with approximately 8cm excess on each side. Drivers repositioning for knot tying.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good suture length management. Appropriate excess for knot formation.

frame_analyses.description: Right hand wrapping suture around left needle driver. Creating first wrap for surgeon's knot (double throw). Suture tension appears controlled.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Right hand leading the wrap sequence. Wrapping technique appears clean. Preparing for second wrap.

frame_analyses.description: Second wrap of surgeon's knot visible. Right hand completing double wrap around left driver. Knot formation appears secure and properly constructed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Double throw complete. Knot appears tight and well-formed. Suture tension maintained throughout wrap sequence.

frame_analyses.description: Instruments repositioning. Left hand now initiating wrap around right needle driver for second throw (single throw). Hand switch from first throw is evident.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear hand reversal from first throw. Left hand now leading. Single wrap being executed.

frame_analyses.description: Right hand wrapping around left driver for third throw. Hand has switched back from second throw. Single wrap in progress.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switching protocol being followed correctly. Third throw initiated with proper hand alternation.

frame_analyses.description: Completed knot visible on drain. Suture tails extending from knot. Scissors not yet visible in frame. Preparing for suture cutting phase.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot appears secure and properly cinched. Drain slit alignment cannot be fully assessed from angle, but knot sits at apparent target location.

frame_analyses.description: Final frame shows completed knot on drain with suture ends still visible. Knot position appears centered on drain slit. No visible gaps in drain closure.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot security appears maintained. Drain slit approximation looks adequate.

phase_timings.benchmark_comparison: expert (10-20s expert range; video shows expedited placement)
expert (10-18s expert range; slightly faster than typical expert)

penalties.description: Entry and exit points of suture relative to marked targets cannot be precisely determined from oblique camera angle. Visible thread path through drain appears approximately aligned with intended marks.
penalties.rationale: Camera angle prevents direct perpendicular view of entry/exit points. Deviation appears minimal but cannot be measured with confidence.

penalties.description: Drain slit appears fully approximated by completed knot. No visible gap observed in slit closure.
penalties.rationale: Magnified view of drain shows knot seated across slit. Full slit closure cannot be completely confirmed from oblique angle.

penalties.description: All three throws executed and knot appears secure. No slipping or failure observed.
Hand switching between throws verified: First throw (right leads), second throw (left leads), third throw (right leads). Protocol correctly followed.
Drain remains secured on block throughout. No avulsion or tearing observed.

knot_assessments.notes: Clean double wrap technique. Right hand executes both wraps around left driver. Knot tension appropriate and maintained.
Left hand leads wrap around right driver. Single wrap cleanly executed. Proper hand alternation from throw 1.
Right hand leads wrap around left driver. Single wrap executed. Proper hand alternation from throw 2. Knot fully secured.

suture_placement.rationale: Oblique camera angle prevents accurate measurement of entry/exit deviation from marked lines. No gross misalignment visible, but precise millimeter deviation cannot be quantified.

confidence_rationale: Completion time and knot quality are clearly observable and well-supported (confidence 0.85-0.95 for those metrics). However, suture placement deviation cannot be measured precisely due to oblique camera angle (confidence 0.35 for entry/exit point alignment). Drain slit closure appears complete but angle prevents perfect perpendicular visualization (confidence 0.6). Overall score confidence reflects strong performance on observable metrics but uncertainty regarding placement precision.
cannot_determine: Exact millimeter deviation of suture entry point from mark 1 — camera angle is oblique to entry surface
Exact millimeter deviation of suture exit point from mark 2 — camera angle does not provide perpendicular view
Whether suture entries are precisely perpendicular or at angle — oblique view obscures approach angle
Complete drain slit closure — knot appears to approximate slit fully, but magnified view does not show cross-section
technique_summary: Expert-level intracorporeal suturing performance. Needle pre-loaded on driver, enabling rapid suture placement (7s total, vs 10-20s expert benchmark). All three knots executed cleanly with proper double-throw surgeon's knot followed by two single throws with correct hand alternation. Completion time of 31.5 seconds is significantly faster than expert proficiency target (112s), suggesting either an abbreviated video segment or exceptionally rapid technique. Knot security is high and drain slit approximation appears complete.
strengths: Exceptionally rapid task completion (31.5s vs 80-120s expert benchmark)
Perfect hand switching protocol across all three throws
Clean, secure knot formation without slipping

demonstrated_techniques: Bilateral needle driver positioning for intracorporeal knot tying
Surgeon's knot (double throw) construction with right hand lead
Single throw execution with left hand lead (throw 2)
Single throw execution with right hand lead (throw 3)
Hand alternation protocol for three-throw sequence

improvement_suggestions: If completion time reflects full task (not truncated video), verify that all suture ends were cut per task requirements — final frames do not clearly show cutting phase
Confirm suture placement is within 2mm of marked targets; although visually aligned, precise measurement from oblique angle not possible
Verify drain slit is fully approximated by performing cross-sectional inspection if possible
video_integrity_notes: Video appears to show only partial sequence. Frames progress through knot tying but do not clearly show final suture cutting with scissors (required for task completion per rubric). Reported completion time of 31.5s is approximately 2.5× faster than expert proficiency benchmark, suggesting either frame selection or video truncation. If this is the complete performance, cutting phase may have been omitted from provided frames.
